shared_properties: The pickup keeps a compact 24x24px footprint and a centered collectible silhouette in every frame.
The accent highlight stays bright enough to read over the warm background palette.
The glow ring remains secondary to the berry body so the collectible feels solid and clickable.
differences: The berry uses a wine-red body to distinguish reward objects from branches and leaf clusters.
A soft glow ring and bobbing loop make the pickup readable during high-speed fall segments.
The silhouette is intentionally circular to contrast with the player leaf and the horizontal branch obstacles.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_gdd
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the core loop, timing windows, obstacle pacing, UI layout, and Bevy target constraints.

reference_images.label: leaf_beat_moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the sepia autumn atmosphere, tree silhouettes, and warm color layering.

bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: TextureAtlas pickup entity with a compact bobbing loop and a circle-friendly overlap collider.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load obstacles/obstacles_fruit_pickup_floating_6f_10fps.png during stage setup and pair it with a small overlap collider for collection checks.
runtime_notes: Animate the pickup at 10fps with a gentle bob to make reward opportunities visible during fast descent.
Keep the overlap collider within a 20x20px center region so collection feels generous without hiding the reward shape.
Spawn the pickup on non-overlapping lanes at roughly 300px depth intervals according to the stage generator.

context.usage: Collectible reward that restores HP up to the cap and adds bonus score.

shape.silhouette: Round berry with a tiny stem, bright highlight, and a warm glow ring.

acceptance_criteria: Sprite sheet is exactly 144x24px with 6 frames of 24x24px and 0px spacing.
The berry body remains centered and fully readable in every frame while the bobbing motion stays inside the 24x24px bounds.
A bright highlight using #F5D060 or #F5ECD7 remains visible on every frame to separate the pickup from branch tones.
